instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `require-all` that evaluates a value once and signals an error if it fails any of the given predicate names; otherwise returns the value.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 X))
            (UNLESS (NUMBERP #:G1) (ERROR "~A failed ~A" #:G1 'NUMBERP))
            (UNLESS (PLUSP #:G1) (ERROR "~A failed ~A" #:G1 'PLUSP))
            (UNLESS (INTEGERP #:G1) (ERROR "~A failed ~A" #:G1 'INTEGERP))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 X))
            (UNLESS (NUMBERP #:G112) (ERROR "~A failed ~A" #:G112 'NUMBERP))
            (UNLESS (PLUSP #:G112) (ERROR "~A failed ~A" #:G112 'PLUSP))
            (UNLESS (INTEGERP #:G112) (ERROR "~A failed ~A" #:G112 'INTEGERP))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
